title: Stieg-Fan
content:
{{Top|fca|leg|notoc=1}} {{Organization |image= |name=Stieg-Fan |type= |founder= |leader= |subgroup= |headquarters= |locations=[[Stieg]]''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Fight for Truth]]'' |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Stieg has no organized government or ruling system. Tribes are locked in constant warfare.''"
"''Hold on. The Stieg-Fan are peaceful and fun-loving. And Stieg has a perfectly fine system of government!|[[V-Davi]] and [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]]|}} The '''Stieg-Fan''' were the tribes living on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stieg]], which was located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} They were known of in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[43 BBY/Legends|43 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' and were generally considered to be an irenic and sociable group with a stable government. However, on the planet [[Kegan/Legends|Kegan]], another world in the Calaron sector, the Stieg-Fan were part of a [[The Learning|propaganda system]] implemented in the [[Learning Circle|local school system]] designed to portray the wider galaxy as dangerous. As part of this effort, the Stieg-Fan were described as locked in constant warfare between tribes, with no provision for the rule of law. ==Behind the scenes== The Stieg-Fan were created by [[Jude Watson]] for her young adult novel ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Fight for Truth]]'', published by [[Scholastic]] in [[2000]]. In this novel, they received a single mention to illustrate the Kegan system of indoctrination, but it is unclear whether they are a species or a culture. ==Appearances== *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Fight for Truth]]'' {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Undefined social groups]]
